nodes.name: ShgoGlobalOptimization
nodes.description: Finds the global minimum of a scalar function using Simplicial Homology Global Optimization (SHGO): iteratively samples the bounded search space via simplicial or quasi-random methods, builds a simplicial complex to locate local minima candidates, and refines each candidate with a local minimizer.

inputs.name: func
inputs.type_desc: callable — scalar objective f(x, *args)

inputs.name: bounds
inputs.type_desc: sequence of (min, max) pairs

inputs.name: args
inputs.type_desc: tuple — extra positional arguments forwarded to func
inputs.constraints: valid tuple — extra positional arguments forwarded to func

inputs.name: constraints
inputs.type_desc: dict or sequence of dicts — equality/inequality constraints

inputs.name: n
inputs.type_desc: int — number of sampling points per iteration

inputs.name: iters
inputs.type_desc: int — number of simplicial complex construction iterations

inputs.name: callback
inputs.type_desc: callable invoked after each iteration with current OptimizeResult
inputs.constraints: valid callable invoked after each iteration with current OptimizeResult

inputs.name: minimizer_kwargs
inputs.type_desc: dict — keyword arguments forwarded to the local minimizer
inputs.constraints: valid dict — keyword arguments forwarded to the local minimizer

inputs.name: options
inputs.type_desc: dict — algorithm-level hyper-parameters

inputs.name: sampling_method
inputs.type_desc: str or callable — sampling strategy
inputs.constraints: one of 'simplicial', 'halton', 'sobol', or a custom callable

outputs.name: result
outputs.type_desc: OptimizeResult — x (optimal point), fun (optimal value), xl (local minima), funl, success, message, nit, nfev

nodes.type_signature: (func: callable — scalar objective f(x, *args), bounds: sequence of (min, max) pairs, args: tuple — extra positional arguments forwarded to func, constraints: dict or sequence of dicts — equality/inequality constraints, n: int — number of sampling points per iteration, iters: int — number of simplicial complex construction iterations, callback: callable invoked after each iteration with current OptimizeResult, minimizer_kwargs: dict — keyword arguments forwarded to the local minimizer, options: dict — algorithm-level hyper-parameters, sampling_method: str or callable — sampling strategy) -> OptimizeResult — x (optimal point), fun (optimal value), xl (local minima), funl, success, message, nit, nfev
nodes.conceptual_summary: TopologicalSamplingGlobalExtremaLocator. Partitions a bounded N-dimensional real-valued search domain into a simplicial complex by iteratively placing quasi-random or structured sample points, then exploits the topological connectivity of that complex to enumerate distinct local-minima basins of attraction for a scalar-valued objective. Each identified basin candidate is subsequently refined via a gradient-based or derivative-free local descent, yielding both the globally optimal point and a ranked catalogue of all local optima discovered within the bounded region.. Applications: Computational Chemistry / Molecular Conformation: locating the globally lowest-energy 3D atomic arrangement of a molecule by minimizing a potential energy surface over torsion-angle and bond-length degrees of freedom — the multi-modal energy landscape with many local minima is precisely the regime where topological basin enumeration outperforms gradient-only methods, Aerospace Trajectory Optimization: finding the globally fuel-optimal sequence of thrust vectors and burn timings for an orbital transfer maneuver, where the scalar objective is total delta-V and the bounded domain covers admissible burn windows and attitude angles — constraint support handles no-fly zones and dynamic-pressure limits, Hyperparameter Tuning for Machine Learning Pipelines: minimizing a scalar validation-loss surface over a bounded continuous hyperparameter space (learning rate, regularization coefficients, architectural ratios) where the loss landscape is non-convex and expensive to evaluate — low-discrepancy sampling maximizes information per evaluation and the local-refinement stage polishes each promising configuration, Geophysical Seismic Inversion: recovering the globally best-fitting subsurface velocity model by minimizing the residual between observed and synthetic seismic waveforms over a bounded space of layer thicknesses and acoustic impedances — the highly multi-modal misfit function (cycle-skipping) benefits from exhaustive basin cataloguing before committing to local gradient descent

nodes.name: DifferentialEvolutionOptimization
nodes.description: Finds the global minimum of a scalar function using Differential Evolution: maintains a population of candidate vectors, applies stochastic mutation and crossover each generation to explore the bounded search space, enforces optional constraints, supports parallel fitness evaluation, and optionally polishes the final candidate with a gradient-based local optimizer.

inputs.name: func
inputs.type_desc: callable — scalar objective f(x, *args)

inputs.name: bounds
inputs.type_desc: sequence of (min, max) pairs or scipy Bounds object

inputs.name: args
inputs.type_desc: tuple — extra positional arguments forwarded to func
inputs.constraints: valid tuple — extra positional arguments forwarded to func

inputs.name: strategy
inputs.type_desc: str — mutation/recombination strategy name

inputs.name: maxiter
inputs.type_desc: int — maximum number of generations

inputs.name: popsize
inputs.type_desc: int — population size multiplier (pop = popsize * len(x))

inputs.name: tol
inputs.type_desc: float — relative convergence tolerance on population spread

inputs.name: mutation
inputs.type_desc: float or (float, float) — differential weight F or dithering range

inputs.name: recombination
inputs.type_desc: float — crossover probability CR

inputs.name: seed
inputs.type_desc: int, np.random.RandomState, np.random.Generator, or None — RNG seed

inputs.name: callback
inputs.type_desc: callable invoked after each generation with xk and convergence

inputs.name: disp
inputs.type_desc: bool — print convergence messages each generation

inputs.name: polish
inputs.type_desc: bool — refine best result with L-BFGS-B after evolution

inputs.name: init
inputs.type_desc: str or array-like — population initialization method or explicit population

inputs.name: atol
inputs.type_desc: float — absolute convergence tolerance on population spread

inputs.name: updating
inputs.type_desc: str — controls when the best vector is updated within a generation

inputs.name: workers
inputs.type_desc: int or map-like — parallel fitness evaluation workers
inputs.constraints: -1 uses all CPU cores; map-like must conform to Pool.map interface

inputs.name: constraints
inputs.type_desc: NonlinearConstraint, LinearConstraint, or sequence thereof

inputs.name: x0
inputs.type_desc: array-like — initial guess seeded into the initial population

outputs.name: result
outputs.type_desc: OptimizeResult — x (optimal point), fun (optimal value), nit, nfev, success, message, population, population_energies

nodes.type_signature: (func: callable — scalar objective f(x, *args), bounds: sequence of (min, max) pairs or scipy Bounds object, args: tuple — extra positional arguments forwarded to func, strategy: str — mutation/recombination strategy name, maxiter: int — maximum number of generations, popsize: int — population size multiplier (pop = popsize * len(x)), tol: float — relative convergence tolerance on population spread, mutation: float or (float, float) — differential weight F or dithering range, recombination: float — crossover probability CR, seed: int, np.random.RandomState, np.random.Generator, or None — RNG seed, callback: callable invoked after each generation with xk and convergence, disp: bool — print convergence messages each generation, polish: bool — refine best result with L-BFGS-B after evolution, init: str or array-like — population initialization method or explicit population, atol: float — absolute convergence tolerance on population spread, updating: str — controls when the best vector is updated within a generation, workers: int or map-like — parallel fitness evaluation workers, constraints: NonlinearConstraint, LinearConstraint, or sequence thereof, x0: array-like — initial guess seeded into the initial population) -> OptimizeResult — x (optimal point), fun (optimal value), nit, nfev, success, message, population, population_energies
nodes.conceptual_summary: StochasticPopulationBasedBoundedGlobalMinimizer. Maintains a finite ensemble of N-dimensional real-valued candidate vectors within a bounded hyperrectangular search space, iteratively perturbing each candidate via weighted difference vectors drawn from the population (mutation) combined with probabilistic component-wise recombination, retaining only candidates that reduce the scalar objective value (selection). After stochastic convergence — measured by relative and absolute spread across the population ensemble — optionally applies a gradient-assisted local descent to the globally best candidate, projecting the population's explorative solution into a locally optimal point within the feasibility region defined by linear and nonlinear constraints.. Applications: Structural engineering — topology and shape optimization of load-bearing lattice structures where the compliance (deformation energy) surface is multimodal and finite-element evaluations are expensive, with geometric manufacturability constraints encoded as nonlinear inequality constraints, Computational chemistry / pharmacology — fitting empirical interatomic potential energy surface parameters (Lennard-Jones, Morse, ReaxFF) to ab-initio quantum mechanical reference data, where the residual sum-of-squares objective is highly non-convex over the bounded parameter hyperrectangle, Geophysical inversion — inferring subsurface elastic modulus and density layer profiles from seismic travel-time residuals, where the misfit functional has numerous local minima corresponding to physically implausible velocity models, and bounds encode prior geological constraints, Hyperparameter optimization in machine learning — tuning bounded continuous and ordinal hyperparameters (learning rate, regularization coefficient, architectural ratios) of a neural network by treating validation loss as a black-box scalar objective with no accessible gradient
